## 📋 Pull Request Memo
 
> *"Before this gets approved, someone has to read it. That someone is you, first."*
 
---
 
### What does this PR do?
A clear and concise description of the change. What was added, updated, or removed?
 
### Why is this change needed?
What problem does it solve or what improvement does it bring to the Corporate Survivors ecosystem?
 
### Type of change
- [ ] 📝 Documentation (new or updated content, corrections)
- [ ] 🎨 Brand / Design asset
- [ ] ⚙️ Repository infrastructure (CI, templates, config)
- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (broken link, formatting error, incorrect information)
- [ ] 🏛️ Governance or process update
- [ ] Other: ___
### Related issue(s)
Closes # *(link the issue this PR resolves, if applicable)*
 
---
 
### ✅ Pre-submission Checklist
 
Before submitting, confirm the following:
 
- [ ] I have read the [Contributing Guide](../CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] My changes follow the tone and voice defined in the [Brand Book](../brand-book/)
- [ ] I have checked for broken links and formatting issues
- [ ] I have not introduced any content that violates the [Code of Conduct](../CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [ ] If this changes a document that cross-references others, I have updated those references too
- [ ] If this affects tokenomics, governance, or security content, I have flagged it clearly below
---
 
### ⚠️ Sensitive Change Flag *(complete if applicable)*
 
Does this PR affect any of the following?
 
- [ ] Tokenomics figures or allocation details
- [ ] Governance Charter or decision-making process
- [ ] Security Policy or incident response procedures
- [ ] Risk Disclosure
- [ ] Official contract address or on-chain verification links
- [ ] Brand assets (logos, colors, mascot)
If any of the above are checked, provide additional context here:
 
---
 
### Additional notes
Anything else the reviewer should know before approving this PR?
 
---
 
> *"Good PRs are like good memos: specific, complete, and unlikely to cause a follow-up meeting."*
>
> — Management
